The one habit that separates traders who last from traders who blow up.

Cut losers fast — that's your stop. Let winners breathe — that's your target.

📌 SAVE this and read it before your next trade.

#XAUUSD #gold #forex #goldtrading

The most expensive sentence in trading is only three words long.

"Fixed returns. Zero risk."

A man said that to me once. He didn't show me charts — he showed me clean, beautiful numbers. In my company I audited every invoice and read every contract twice. But that one sentence walked straight past five years of business instinct, because it promised the one thing business never gave me: certainty.

I handed him three years of profit. A few months later the numbers were gone, and so was he.

If anyone ever promises you fixed, guaranteed, or risk-free — walk away. That's the whole lesson, and it cost me everything to learn it.

The full story: https://rextradingsignal.com/about.html

Rule 01 of this channel: every signal has a stop loss. Before we ever talk about profit.

Why so strict? Because capital is oxygen. No oxygen, no next trade.

A stop loss isn't admitting defeat. It's the price of staying in the game long enough for your edge to show up. The trader who survives a bad week is the one still here for the good one.

I learned this the hard way — my first blown account had no stop loss, because a stop "felt like giving up." The market gave up on me instead.

Decide what you're willing to lose before you click. Every time.

How much should you risk on one gold trade? 1–2%. That's it.

At 1% you can lose 20 in a row and still keep 80% of your account. That's how you survive.

📌 SAVE this number. It matters more than any signal.

What is this candle telling you? 🕯️

Every candle tells a story:
• Body = open price → close price
• Wick (the tail) = the highest/lowest price it touched
• Green = price went up 🟢 | Red = price went down 🔴

Learn this and you can read ANY chart.

📌 Save this so you never forget when you open a chart!

#XAUUSD #gold #forex #goldtrading #tradingbasics

Position sizing sounds like maths homework. It's one division.

Take the amount you're willing to lose on a trade. Divide it by the distance to your stop loss. That's your size. Done.

Here's the part beginners get backwards: a WIDER stop means a SMALLER trade, not a bigger one. More room for the market to breathe = fewer units, so your dollar risk stays fixed.

Get this one habit right and you'll never again "accidentally" risk half your account on a single gold trade.

Position sizing without the maths headache: https://rextradingsignal.com/position-sizing-for-gold-trading.html

Why do most traders blow their accounts? It's almost never the strategy.

I burned four accounts finding out. Four different ways to lose:

No plan — I bought gold because it "looked strong."
Revenge — I doubled size after every loss to get back to even faster.
The guru — I followed a man who only posted his winners, blind.
Overconfidence — a few good months, so I sized up until one news spike erased six.

Different mistakes, one root cause: I traded my emotions instead of a written rule.

The strategy was never the problem. The discipline was.

The four failure patterns, in full: https://rextradingsignal.com/why-do-most-traders-blow-their-accounts.html

The one number that decides if you survive: risk-to-reward.

At 1:3 you can be WRONG more than half the time and still profit. Pros chase the ratio, not the win rate.

Treat trading like a business and most of your problems disappear.

A business has: a written plan, a spending limit, a ledger, and a review at the end of the day. Trading needs the exact same four:

A written trade plan — so you're not improvising with money.
A risk ceiling — your spending limit, decided while calm.
A trade journal — your ledger of what actually happened.
A nightly audit — the review that turns mistakes into lessons.

The moment I ran my trades the way I ran my company, the account stopped feeling like a casino.

When a BIG candle completely "swallows" the small one before it… 😳

Control just changed hands:
• Big green swallows red → BUYERS win 🟢
• Big red swallows green → SELLERS win 🔴

Strongest when it shows up at support or resistance.

Account number two died of revenge.

After a loss, I doubled my size to get back to even faster. Then doubled again. Three trades — that's all it took to close the account.

Revenge trading feels like fighting back. It's actually the market handing you a bigger shovel.

Here's the fix, and it's boring: your size never changes because of the last trade's result. Same risk when you're up, same risk when you're down. The trade in front of you doesn't know or care what the last one did.

Flat, unemotional size is how an account survives a losing streak — and every trader gets one.

Rule 03: I never promise profit. Here's why I can't, and won't.

Account number three died because I paid a "guru" who only ever posted his winners. I followed him blindly. When the losing streak came — it always comes — I had no idea why I was in any of my trades, so I couldn't manage a single one.

A track record you can't see through is worthless. Promises of "guaranteed" and "fixed" are the loudest sign someone is hiding the losing half.

So I'll say it plainly: I don't know if the next trade wins. Nobody does. What I can promise is a stop loss on every one, and the losses posted alongside the wins.

That's the only honest deal in this business.

That long "tail" on a candle = a REJECTION 🚫

The longer the tail, the harder price was pushed back:
• Long tail below → buyers rejected lower prices → likely UP
• Long tail above → sellers rejected higher prices → likely DOWN

The best trade I made last week was the one I didn't take.

The setup was "almost" there. Almost is a no. So I closed the laptop.

Amateurs force trades out of boredom. Professionals wait — sometimes all day — for the one setup that fits their rules.

You don't get paid for activity. You get paid for precision. Wait for your pitch.

This candle means the market is… UNDECIDED 🤔

Open and close are almost the same → nobody won.
Don't rush to buy or sell. WAIT for the next candle to confirm.

Patience beats guessing.

💬 Do you "wait" or "jump in"? Type WAIT or IN below!

#XAUUSD #gold #doji #candlestick #forex

More trades does not mean more money. Usually the opposite.

Every extra trade you take out of boredom or FOMO is a fresh chance to lose. Ten mediocre trades beat you up. One clean trade pays you.

If you're trading every hour, you're not trading — you're gambling with extra steps.

Do less. Choose better.

Say it with me: no stop loss = no trade.

The stop isn't admitting defeat — it's the price of staying in the game long enough for your edge to show up.

Set it when you're calm. Then honor it when you're scared.

Most traders do the exact opposite of what works.

They snatch tiny profits fast (scared to lose them) and let losers run (hoping they come back). Small wins, big losses. That math kills accounts.

Flip it: cut losers fast — that's your stop. Let winners breathe — that's your target. Boring rule. Life-changing results.

The moment you move your stop loss further away "just to give it room"… you've stopped trading and started hoping.

A stop is a promise you make to yourself when you're calm. Moving it is breaking that promise when you're scared.

The number one account killer isn't a bad strategy. It's a stop loss that keeps sliding.

The second you trade what you NEED instead of what you SEE, you've already lost.

Gold doesn't care that you're down this month. Trade the chart in front of you.

How much should you risk on one gold trade? 1–2% of your account. That's it.

On $1,000 that's $10–$20 per trade. Sounds small. It's supposed to.

At 1%, you could lose 20 in a row and still keep 80% of your account. At 20% risk, three bad trades and you're done.

Survival first. Everything else is second.

The one number that decides if you SURVIVE or blow up 💥

Risk $20 to make $60 (a 1:3 ratio).
That means you can be WRONG 6 out of 10 times and still profit.

Pros don't chase "being right often" — they chase "winning bigger than they lose."

Before you risk one real dollar on gold, trade a demo for a month.

Not to prove you can win — to prove you can FOLLOW YOUR RULES. Stop loss every time. Same risk every time. Journal every trade.

If you can't stay disciplined with fake money, real money will wreck you. The demo isn't beneath you. It's the exam.

Good trading is boring. One or two clean setups, then you close the laptop.

The traders glued to the screen all day aren't disciplined — they're emotional.

🔁 SHARE if your best trades were your calmest ones.

#XAUUSD #gold #forex #trading

Big news drops (NFP, CPI) and gold goes vertical. Feels like free money. It's a trap.

Spreads widen. Price whips both ways. Your stop can get skipped entirely. This is where accounts die in seconds.

When the news hits, the professional move is often to do nothing. Let the chaos pass. Trade the clean chart after — not the explosion.

Quick gold tip: keep one eye on the US Dollar (DXY).

Gold and the dollar usually move opposite ways. Dollar up, gold tends down — and vice versa.

If you're long gold while the dollar is ripping higher, you're swimming against the current. Not a hard rule, but a strong hint. Context beats guessing.

How much should you trade? Just one division 🧮

Position size = money you'll risk ÷ stop-loss distance

The WIDER your stop → the SMALLER your trade
(so your dollar risk always stays the same)

Your trade moves nicely in your favor. Now what?

Move your stop loss to your entry (break-even). Now the worst case is: you lose nothing.

A trade that can't lose is a beautiful thing. You stop trading scared and start letting the winner run. Protect first, profit second.

Do your thinking BEFORE the money's on the line.

At night, calm: what setup, what stop, what target, how much risk? Then morning-you just follows the plan.

"I win 70% of my trades!" Cool. Are you profitable? Often the answer is no.

If your winners are tiny and your losers are huge, a 70% win rate still bleeds you dry.

Stop counting how OFTEN you're right. Start measuring how BIG you win versus how small you lose. That ratio is the whole game.

Set your "LOSS CEILING" before you get emotional 🧯

While you're calm, decide: the MOST I'll lose this month.
Then break it down: month → week → day → per trade.

The market can't blow your account past a ceiling you set in advance. Only you can.

💬 What's your monthly loss ceiling in %? Drop it in the comments!

Nobody wants to hear it, but the fastest way to grow an account is slowly.

2% a week doesn't sound sexy. Compounded over a year, it's life-changing.

The traders chasing 100% a month are usually at 0% by year end — or below. Aim for steady. Let compounding do the flashy part quietly.

The "floor" and the "ceiling" of price 🏠

• Resistance (ceiling) = where sellers keep stepping in → price struggles to rise
• Support (floor) = where buyers keep defending → price struggles to fall

Price bounces between them. Trade AT the level, not in no-man's-land.

When a trend starts to CRACK… ⚠️

Price was rising nicely (higher highs & lows), then it closes BELOW the last higher low → Break of Structure (BOS).

It's an EARLY warning: control is shifting. Don't ignore it.
